summary:
**1. Input Query:** identity theft in Pakistan --- ### 2. Source Links Referenced for Analysis | # | URL (onion) | Brief Description | |---|-------------|-------------------| | 1 | `http://juhanurmihxlp77nkq76byazcldy2hlmovfu2epvl5ankdibsot4csyd.onion/` | Ahmia index page warning of a man‑in‑the‑middle fake clone. | | 2 | `http://3bbad7fauom4d6sgppalyqddsqbf5u5p56b5k5uk2zxsy3d6ey2jobad.onion/` | OnionLand search engine landing page (service index). | | 3 | `http://dwebkjkovsjobzrb45dz6prnlifnapiyp2dba33vcmcsaikr2re4d5qd.onion/` | OnionLand Tor‑hosting service (payment methods, crypto‑only). | | 4 | `http://i2poulge3qyo33q4uazlda367okpkczn4rno2vjfetawoghciae6ygad.onion/` | I2P search engine (mirrors of hidden services). | | 5 | `http://drdj7jgn5nrnp4ebxgi36dy55uamqyow2tghbzinqmbazkkukb4lcaqd.onion/western-union-identity-theft-scam/index.html` | “Western Union Identity Theft Scam” tutorial page (Mar 2021). | | 6 | `http://goodgkavjfqzqpshpj34q3tphzv5v6fspoqsjk7mdrduv6dihgeeb3id.onion/tag/how-to-make-scampages/index.html` | “How to make scampages” – phishing/scam‑page tutorial (Blackhat Pakistan). | | 7 | `http://goodgkavjfqzqpshpj34q3tphzv5v6fspoqsjk7mdrduv6dihgeeb3id.onion/tag/phishing/index.html` | Phishing tag page – collection of phishing guides (Blackhat Pakistan). | | 8 | `http://goodgkavjfqzqpshpj34q3tphzv5v6fspoqsjk7mdrduv6dihgeeb3id.onion/tag/spamming/index.html` | Spamming tag page – guides & tools (Blackhat Pakistan). | | 9 | `http://goodgkavjfqzqpshpj34q3tphzv5v6fspoqsjk7mdrduv6dihgeeb3id.onion/tag/how-to-make-fake-page/index.html` | “How to make fake page” – step‑by‑step phishing page creation (Blackhat Pakistan). | |10| `http://goodgkavjfqzqpshpj34q3tphzv5v6fspoqsjk7mdrduv6dihgeeb3id.onion/category/all-about-spamming/scampages-free-download/index.html` | Repository of downloadable “scampage” templates (Blackhat Pakistan). | --- ### 3. Investigation Artifacts | Artifact Type | Value / Description | Context Seen | |---------------|---------------------|--------------| | **Forum / Site Name** | **Blackhat Pakistan** (dark‑web marketplace / tutorial hub) | Multiple “how‑to” pages (phishing, scampages, spamming) – all hosted on the same `.onion` domain (goodgkavjfqzqpshpj34q3tphzv5v6fspoqsjk7mdrduv6dihgeeb3id.onion). | | **Article Title** | “Western Union Identity Theft Scam” | `drdj7jgn5nrnp4ebxgi36dy55uamqyow2tghbzinqmbazkkukb4lcaqd.onion` – describes a “Western Union hack” used for identity theft. | | **Author / Pseudonym** | “admin” (generic) – no personal identifier. | | **Date of Publication** | 28 Mar 2021 (post timestamp). | | **Keywords / TTPs** | *Identity theft, Western Union hack, SSN, Visa data, “Western Union free money hack”, data harvesting, forum posting.* | | **Cryptocurrency Payment Options** | BTC, Lightning, ETH, XMR, USDT, USDC (listed on OnionLand hosting page). | Indicates that services/tools are monetised via crypto, typical for illicit ops. | | **Service Offering** | Tor‑hosting (custom v3 vanity onion, WordPress, LAMP stack) – no KYC, crypto‑only. | Could be used to host phishing/scampage sites targeting Pakistani victims. | | **Malware / Tool Names** | “Scampage” (template for fake login pages), “Fake BTC sender Tool”, “Android Hacking Course”, “Keyloggers”, “Botnets”, “CRYPTER”. | All listed for sale on Blackhat Pakistan marketplace. | | **Targeted Geography** | Repeated reference to “Pakistan” in site branding (Blackhat Pakistan) and in tutorial language (e.g., “Blackhat Pakistan” tag). | | **Attack Vectors** | Phishing pages (scampages), spamming campaigns, Western Union money‑transfer fraud, credential harvesting. | | **Potential Victim Data Types** | Social Security / National ID numbers, bank account numbers, credit‑card PANs, Visa data, login credentials for banking/Western Union. | | **Related Infrastructure** | Search engine mirrors (Ahmia, OnionLand, I2P) – useful for locating additional hidden services. | | **Indicators of Compromise (IOCs)** | - Onion address of tutorial hub: `goodgkavjfqzqpshpj34q3tphzv5v6fspoqsjk7mdrduv6dihgeeb3id.onion`
- Hosting service onion: `dwebkjkovsjobzrb45dz6prnlifnapiyp2dba33vcmcsaikr2re4d5qd.onion`
- Scam tutorial onion: `drdj7jgn5nrnp4ebxgi36dy55uamqyow2tghbzinqmbazkkukb4lcaqd.onion` | | **Payment Crypto Addresses** | Not explicitly listed in the scraped text, but the hosting page states acceptance of BTC, ETH, XMR, USDT, USDC – suggests wallet addresses are likely displayed on the live site (not captured in the excerpt). | --- ### 4. Key Insights | # | Insight | Why it matters (data‑driven) | |---|---------|------------------------------| | 1 | **A dedicated Pakistani‑focused dark‑web tutorial hub (Blackhat Pakistan) is actively publishing step‑by‑step guides for creating phishing “scampages” and for Western Union‑based identity theft.** | The presence of multiple “how‑to” pages under the same onion domain demonstrates an organized knowledge‑sharing operation targeting local (Pakistani) victims. | | 2 | **The same infrastructure (Tor‑hosting service) is advertised to host these illicit pages, with crypto‑only payment and no KYC.** | Attackers can quickly spin up anonymous phishing sites (vanity .onion addresses) and monetize via crypto, reducing traceability. | | 3 | **Western Union is specifically highlighted as a high‑value fraud vector, with instructions to harvest SSN/Visa data and post it on “Western Union hackers forum”.** | Indicates a shift from generic credential phishing to money‑transfer fraud, which can lead to rapid cash‑out via Western Union’s global network. | | 4 | **The marketplace sells ready‑made “scampage” templates and auxiliary tools (keyloggers, botnets, crypto‑sender tools).** | Lowers the barrier to entry for less‑skilled actors, potentially increasing the volume of phishing attacks originating from Pakistan. | | 5 | **Search engine mirrors (Ahmia, OnionLand, I2P) list the same onion addresses, confirming the persistence and discoverability of these resources.** | Enables threat hunters to set up automated monitoring of these indexes for new content or changes (e.g., new tutorial releases). | --- ### 5. Next Steps | Action | Suggested Query / Method | |--------|--------------------------| | **Monitor the Blackhat Pakistan onion** for new posts or tool releases. | `site:goodgkavjfqzqpshpj34q3tphzv5v6fspoqsjk7mdrduv6dihgeeb3id.onion "phishing" OR "scampage" OR "Western Union"` | | **Collect live cryptocurrency wallet addresses** from the hosting and tutorial pages for blockchain analysis. | Visit `dwebkjkovsjobzrb45dz6prnlifnapiyp2dba33vcmcsaikr2re4d5qd.onion` and scrape displayed BTC/ETH/XMR addresses; run them through blockchain explorers (e.g., Blockchair, OXT). | | **Identify active scampage URLs** that may be used in ongoing campaigns. | Use a web crawler on the “scampages‑free‑download” page to extract downloadable HTML files; then hash the files and search for matches on public phishing‑site feeds (PhishTank, OpenPhish). | | **Track Western Union fraud chatter** on other dark‑web forums. | Search for “Western Union hack” and “Western Union identity theft” across Ahmia and OnionLand indexes; note any new forum URLs or Discord/Telegram invite links. | | **Set up sink‑hole domains** for known scampage templates to capture victim traffic. | Deploy a controlled .onion service mimicking popular vanity addresses (e.g., those listed in the tutorial) and log HTTP requests for forensic analysis. | | **Engage local CERT/ISAC** in Pakistan with the compiled IOCs (onion addresses, tool names) to raise awareness and improve detection rules. | Provide them with regex patterns for “scampage” URLs and known phishing page signatures. | | **Automate alerts** for any new postings on the hosting service that mention “vanity onion”, “WordPress”, or “no logs”. | Use a periodic scrape of `dwebkjkovsjobzrb45dz6prnlifnapiyp2dba33vcmcsaikr2re4d5qd.onion` and trigger alerts on keyword matches. | --- **Bottom Line:** The dark‑web ecosystem surrounding “Blackhat Pakistan” is a fertile ground for identity‑theft operations, especially those leveraging Western Union fraud and phishing scampages. Continuous monitoring of the identified onion services, extraction of associated crypto wallets, and proactive sink‑hole deployment are essential to disrupt the pipeline and protect potential victims in Pakistan.
